registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/cr.description: A non-cryptographic hash function using AES-NI for high performance
A vector with fixed capacity, backed by an array (it can be stored on the stack too). Implements fixed capacity ArrayVec and ArrayString.
Simple bit field trait providing get_bit, get_bits, set_bit, and set_bits methods for Rust's integral types.
This crate provides macros to generate bitfield-like struct.
A macro to generate structures which behave like bitflags.
A macro to generate structures which behave like bitflags.
A build-time dependency for Cargo build scripts to assist in invoking the native C compiler to compile native C code into a static archive to be linked into Rust code.
A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg] parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the item that gets emitted.
Traits for key comparison in maps.
Find windows-specific tools, read MSVC versions from the registry and from COM interfaces
A Rust port of Google's SwissTable hash map
A Rust port of Google's SwissTable hash map
A hash table with consistent order and fast iteration.
Simple allocator usable for no_std systems. It builds a linked list from the freed blocks and thus needs no additional data structures.
Wrappers to create fully-featured Mutex and RwLock types. Compatible with no_std.
Provides extremely fast (uses SIMD on x86_64, aarch64 and wasm32) routines for 1, 2 or 3 byte search and single substring search.
A unified interface for reading and writing object file formats.
Single assignment cells and lazy values.

A library to parse the x86 CPUID instruction, written in rust with no external dependencies. The implementation closely resembles the Intel CPUID manual description. The library does only depend on libcore.
Path parsing primitives for Redox
A Rust library to access raw Redox system calls
Rust compiler symbol demangling.

A RAII scope guard that will run a given closure when it goes out of scope, even if the code between panics (assuming unwinding panic).  Defines the macros `defer!`, `defer_on_unwind!`, `defer_on_success!` as shorthands for guards with one of the implemented strategies.
A generic serialization/deserialization framework
Serde traits only, with no support for derive -- use the `serde` crate instead
Serde-compatible spanned Value
Split a string into shell words, like Python's shlex.
Pre-allocated storage for a uniform data type
Spin-based synchronization primitives
A simple spinlock crate based on the abstractions provided by `lock_api`.

A native Rust encoder and decoder of TOML-formatted files and streams. Provides implementations of the standard Serialize/Deserialize traits for TOML data to facilitate deserializing and serializing Rust structures.
A TOML-compatible datetime type
Yet another format-preserving TOML parser.
A low-level interface for writing out TOML
Tiny crate to check the version of the installed/running rustc.
A byte-oriented, zero-copy, parser combinators library

Library to program x86 (amd64) hardware. Contains x86 specific data structure descriptions, data-tables, as well as convenience function to call assembly instructions typically not exposed in higher level languages.
Zerocopy makes zero-cost memory manipulation effortless. We write 'unsafe' so you don't have to.
